channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Tree opposite street lamp road marker 28’, 17th May
item.description:
Fly tipping by tree opposite street lamp road marker 28’

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brondesbury Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load

item.title: Overflowing Bins - nothing being done, 17th May
item.description:
This is now my seventh report about the matter, and despite repeatedly being told that action has been taken, the problem has clearly not been resolved. The bins are now overflowing into the street, creating an unacceptable mess and a potential health and safety hazard for residents and pedestrians. It is shocking that this issue continues to be ignored despite multiple reports being made over a significant period of time. I would like a clear explanation as to why previous reports have apparently been marked as completed when no effective action has been taken. Residents should not have to repeatedly chase the council over basic waste collection responsibilities.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Station Approach, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Continuing fly tipping since August 2025, 17th May
item.description:
Lack of refuse facilities for residents results in constant fly tipping

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mackenzie Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytip, 17th May
item.description:
Outside 33- 33a bourdon road there is a load of rubbish that has been there for at least 1 month. It is 2 large cardboard boxies with polystyrene and plastic inside , a garden table , some wood and some seat bases.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bourdon Road, Anerley, Greater London

item.title: Bin bag left on street, 17th May
item.description:
A bin bag was left on the street yesterday evening (fly tipped- when they could have used residents bin stores given that they live in the building) It has now been ripped open overnight and rubbish and food is everywhere

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Westbrook Road, Kidbrooke
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car blocking my driveway., 17th May
item.description:
Car is obstructing my drive way. I am going to struggle to get off my drive for work.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kingswood Avenue, Lessness Heath, Greater London

item.title: Blocked drain leading to flooding, 17th May

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 207, Elmers End Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rats, 17th May
item.description:
I walked through the woods on Friday and Saturday this week and there are a number of rats along the foot path that leads from Parkhill to Murchinson Roads. I see around 8 just sitting on the path at one time - they scurried up the trees when approached. I appreciate there is a river there but seems to be a high number of them. There were also 2 just sitting by the bin at Murchinson entrance.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 34, West Woodside, Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Weller house se16 4uu . Bin room door broken., 17th May
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): East Lane, Bermondsey, Greater London
